repo_prior_art.cite: none; API PartialID is empty, closest repo analogues are finite binary/ordinal bounded-defier proposals

published_prior_art.cite: Klein2010: derives local monotonicity-violation bias terms, but not this exact bounded derivative-feasibility interval

repo_prior_art.cite: none; no in-catalogue continuous-IV zero-envelope entry

published_prior_art.cite: HeckmanVytlacil2005: LIV/MTE derivative formula under threshold crossing

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_ordinal_late_partial_defiers_v1 and pid_dose_response_iv_v1 are finite/adjacent-pasting analogues, not a continuous scalar-IV derivative-envelope sharp set

published_prior_art.cite: BalkePearl1997 gives finite binary-IV sharp bounds; MogstadSantosTorgovitsky2018 gives MTE-policy bounds, neither supplies a continuous negative-first-stage derivative envelope sharp set

repo_prior_art.cite: none; no in-catalogue open-class separation from quasi-monotone continuous-IV formula

published_prior_art.cite: Klein2010: already shows monotonicity departures bias MTE/ATE/LATE estimands; open-set sharp-set exclusion is a stronger formulation

novelty_flags.label: Related work / Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The continuous-LIV comparison misses KennedyLorchSmall2019, JRSSB, doi:10.1111/rssb.12300, which is a closer published continuous-instrument LIV curve comparator even though it retains monotonicity.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship; strict separation from the derivative ratio is too close to Klein2010 unless strengthened into a quantified generic robustness theorem or paired with inference/estimation for Theta_CIV(P,b).

soundness_flags.label: Formal setup / Assumption signed-response
soundness_flags.one_line: The locked parameters advertise only bounded outcomes, but 0 <= a_+ <= c_+ and 0 <= a_- <= c_- impose nonnegative treatment-effect numerators; standard LATE/MTE allows signed effects.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The displayed condition max{0,m'(z)-a_+(z)} <= f(z) has the wrong sign and is not equivalent to the stated upper bound a_+(z) <= m'(z)+f(z).

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The sharp program is written only in derivative variables plus a BV path condition; it does not state the boundary/level constraints needed to match the full observable law P, such as p(0), m(0), and outcome-level feasibility.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the numerator restrictions by signed bounds, e.g. -c_+ <= a_+ <= c_+ and -c_- <= a_- <= c_-, or explicitly lock a nonnegative-treatment-effect assumption and narrow all HV comparisons accordingly.
Add boundary and level-matching constraints to Theta_CIV(P,b), separating derivative feasibility from the requirement that one full-data law reproduces the observed P.
Add Kennedy-Lorch-Small continuous LIV and a monotonicity-violation sensitivity comparator to Related work, then state exactly why the derivative-envelope sharp set is not covered.
To plausibly reach flagship tier, add an inference-ready robustness curve or a theorem nesting binary, ordinal, and continuous-IV monotonicity-violation bounds as one limiting object.

recommended_next_step: Revise the signed numerator algebra and full-P feasibility constraints, add the missing continuous-LIV comparator, then rerun novelty review.
